ddls:
CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the request */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Log level (e.g., INFO, ERROR) */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log (e.g., request, error) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Log message */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method (e.g., POST, GET) */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the request */,
  "content_length" INTEGER /* Content length of the request */,
  "remote_address" TEXT /* IP address of the remote client */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the client */,
  "response_code" INTEGER /* Response code of the request */,
  "response_body" TEXT /* Response body of the request */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service where the request was made */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specific endpoint of the request */,
  "request_status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., accepted, ok, failed) */,
  "request_duration" REAL /* Duration of the request in seconds */,
  "bytes_sent" INTEGER /* Bytes sent in the response */,
  "bytes_received" INTEGER /* Bytes received in the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_request_status FOREIGN KEY ("request_status") REFERENCES request_status ("status_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_response_code FOREIGN KEY ("response_code") REFERENCES response_codes ("response_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "health_status" TEXT /* Health status of the service (e.g., operational, degraded, down) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each endpoint */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the endpoint belongs to */,
  "endpoint_name" TEXT /* Name of the endpoint */,
  "endpoint_path" TEXT /* Path of the endpoint */,
  "endpoint_method" TEXT /* HTTP method supported by the endpoint */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, operations) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Password hash of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "request_id" TEXT /* ID of the request being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the request */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access log was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access log was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the status was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the status was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_codes" (
  "response_code" INTEGER /* HTTP response code */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the response code */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response code was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response code was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Log level (e.g., INFO, ERROR) */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log (e.g., request, error) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Log message */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the log is related to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the log */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system event */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the system event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of system event (e.g., startup, shutdown) */,
  "event_message" TEXT /* Message describing the system event */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the system event was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the system event was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_headers" (
  "header_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each header */,
  "request_id" TEXT /* ID of the request the header belongs to */,
  "header_name" TEXT /* Name of the header */,
  "header_value" TEXT /* Value of the header */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the header was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the header was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("header_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_headers_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_configuration" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "config_key" TEXT /* Key of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security incident */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the security incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of security incident (e.g., failed login, suspicious activity) */,
  "incident_message" TEXT /* Message describing the security incident */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the security incident was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the security incident was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)
